description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a blue gable roof and a covered front porch. The exterior walls are constructed from red bricks with white mortar, and the windows are symmetrically arranged with white frames. The roof is steeply pitched and extends over both the main structure and the porch, providing shelter. The house features a prominent entryway with a small overhang supported by posts, and a side porch that is also covered. The windows are evenly distributed on all sides, giving the house a balanced and traditional appearance. The overall design is simple yet functional, suitable for a suburban or urban neighborhood.
description: A two-story red brick house with a blue gable roof, symmetrical white-framed windows, and covered porches at the front and side.
description_view_0: The view shows the side of the house with four windows and a partial view of the side porch under the blue roof.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the gable end with two vertically aligned windows and a clear view of the side porch.
description_view_2: The front view displays the main entryway with a covered porch, two upper windows, and a side porch extending to the right.
description_view_3: This view shows the other gable end with a covered front porch, two lower windows, and one upper window.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered front porch with steps, supported by posts, and a small overhang above the main door.
